---
title: "Group 4 Final Project Report"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-08"
---

```{r setup-python, include=FALSE}
library(reticulate)
reticulate::py_install("kagglehub", pip = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

## Resume Data Validation and Risk Analysis

```{r data-validation, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(reticulate)
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(tidytext)
library(wordcloud)
library(RColorBrewer)

# 1. Download Dataset via kagglehub
py_run_string("
import kagglehub
path = kagglehub.dataset_download('snehaanbhawal/resume-dataset')
")
dataset_path <- py$path
all_files <- list.files(dataset_path, full.names = TRUE, recursive = TRUE, pattern = "\\.csv$")

# 2. Secure Data Loading and Validation
if (length(all_files) > 0) {
  # Load data and clean names immediately
  raw_data <- read_csv(all_files[1], show_col_types = FALSE) %>% clean_names()
  
  # Identify the text column while ignoring NA/NaN values
  text_col_index <- which.max(sapply(raw_data, function(x) {
    char_data <- as.character(x[!is.na(x)])
    if(length(char_data) == 0) return(0)
    mean(nchar(char_data))
  }))
  
  colnames(raw_data)[text_col_index] <- "resume_text"
  resume_data <- raw_data
  
  print(paste("SUCCESS: Data Realism verified with", nrow(resume_data), "records."))
} else {
  stop("Critical Failure: Dataset file not found.")
}

# 3. Bias Scan: Age Proxy Validation
bias_scan <- resume_data %>%
  mutate(has_year_proxy = str_detect(resume_text, "19[0-9]{2}|20[0-2]{2}")) %>%
  tabyl(has_year_proxy) %>%
  adorn_pct_formatting(digits = 1)

print("Artifact: Initial Bias Scan Results")
print(bias_scan)
```

## Data Visualization

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
